data.title: Brainstorm misclassifies COMPLEX epics as MODERATE after context compaction
data.description:
## Problem

After brainstorm completed for dso-qo0x (recipe registry epic), the orchestrator classified it as MODERATE with scope_certainty HIGH and routed it directly to /dso:implementation-plan, skipping /dso:preplanning entirely. The user had to intervene and point out that both dso-qo0x and 8d21-3aa8 were too complex for direct implementation planning.

## Rationalization at the time

The orchestrator stated:

> "Epic dso-qo0x has 8 SCs but is standalone infrastructure with no cross-system integration (no planning/sprint changes, no hook modifications, no safeguard file edits). Engines are well-researched, approach is proven."

> "Classification: MODERATE (scope_certainty: HIGH) — clear deliverables, known engines, realistic fixtures are the main unknown. No safeguard files, no cross-system coordination."

> "Routing: MODERATE + HIGH → /dso:implementation-plan directly (skip full preplanning per brainstorm routing rule)"

This rationalization dismissed the actual complexity indicators:
- 8 success criteria (high SC count)
- 3 unfamiliar engine integrations (ts-morph, Rope, Synvert)
- External integration tests against real open-source repos
- Registry schema with JSON validation
- Execution engine with rollback semantics
- 14 surviving scenarios from scenario analysis

The orchestrator focused on what the epic DOESN'T touch (no safeguard files, no cross-system coordination) to justify a lower classification, rather than evaluating the intrinsic complexity of what it DOES deliver.

## Likely Contributing Factor: Context Compaction

This misclassification occurred after a context compaction event. The brainstorm session was long (full Phase 1-3 for the recipe epic, plus two earlier epics in the same session). By the time the orchestrator reached the classification step, the detailed scenario analysis and research findings that informed the epic spec had likely been compacted out of the active context window.

With the scenario analysis compressed, the orchestrator may have had a shallower view of the epic's complexity — seeing "standalone infrastructure" as the salient characteristic rather than the 14 scenarios and 3 engine integrations that made it complex.

## Expected Behavior

1. The complexity evaluator should weight SC count, engine count, and scenario count as complexity indicators regardless of whether the epic touches safeguard files
2. 8 SCs + 3 unfamiliar engines + 14 surviving scenarios should classify as COMPLEX, not MODERATE
3. Post-compaction classification decisions should be flagged for user review, or the evaluator should re-read the epic spec from the ticket system rather than relying on compressed context

## Affected Epics

- dso-qo0x: Classified MODERATE, should have been COMPLEX
- 8d21-3aa8: Not explicitly classified but implied MODERATE by routing recommendation
